opening.observed_words: hi i just learned there are more applicants on linkedin than there are actual jobs posted
opening.mechanism: Statement of a surprising fact about job applications on LinkedIn

content_arc.audience_problem: Too many applicants on LinkedIn compared to actual job postings
content_arc.progression: Explanation of companies moving away from LinkedIn, introduction of Jobright, highlighting features, and showing examples
content_arc.payoff: Access to hidden job postings and auto-fill application features

cta.requested_action: Comment 'hidden' to receive a link to Jobright

claims.observed_claim: Jobright pulls over 400,000 listings daily from 200,000 company career pages
Jobright's hidden jobs filter shows roles not listed on LinkedIn or Indeed

transferable_structure.hook_pattern: Surprising fact or statistic to grab attention
transferable_structure.beat_pattern: Problem identification, solution introduction, feature highlighting, example demonstration
transferable_structure.payoff_pattern: Call to action with a specific instruction to engage

evidence_limitations: No visual evidence of the Jobright platform interface or user statistics
No independent verification of the number of job listings or users

metadata.speech_exact: hi i just learned there are more applicants on linkedin than there are actual jobs posted which explains a lot
see it turns out companies got tired of getting spanned with hundreds of robo applications
so a lot of them stopped quietly posting on linkedin altogether and started opening on their actual website
which is why a platform called job right now has over 2 million users
it goes directly to company career pages so you don't have to see it
pulls over 400 000 listings every day from 200 000 company career pages
not recycled not reposted fresh but here's where it's actually useful
there's a hidden jobs filter roles that are not on linkedin or indeed at all
which are posted just very recently and have very little applicants
and most people don't even realize this exists see for example there's this devops role at trans market group
posted a few hours ago with less than 25 applicants there's also this analyst role at cgi
also not even on linkedin yet but my favorite part is that it auto fills applications
and it can tailor your resume per job in one click and as someone who's gone through the job search grind
i genuinely wish i I had this earlier anyways guys it is free so just comment hidden
and I'll send you the link

opening.observed_words: Ungatekeeping the 3 sites that got me a $55/h 2026 summer internship
opening.observed_visual: Selfie-style shot of a young woman outdoors, wearing a cap and polo shirt

content_arc.progression: Sequentially revealing three specific websites used for job hunting with ratings and descriptors

claims.observed_claim: Utilizing these three specific platforms directly resulted in securing a high-paying ($55/hour) summer internship for the year 2026.

transferable_structure.hook_pattern: Gatekeeping reversal, positioning the creator as a helpful insider
transferable_structure.beat_pattern: Listicle format with sequential presentation of tools

evidence_limitations: Limited to visual evidence from the video
No audio or speech analysis provided
No context on the creator's background or experience

opening.observed_words: If you haven't gotten a summer internship yet, you need to hear this.
opening.observed_visual: A young man sits at a desk, looking directly at the camera. Text overlay reads: 'NO SUMMER INTERNSHIP? (Why you shouldn't stress)'.
opening.mechanism: Direct address with text overlay hook.

content_arc.audience_problem: Anxiety about not securing a summer internship.
content_arc.progression: Debunking the myth of summer-only internships and presenting fall/spring options as strategic alternatives.
content_arc.payoff: Reassurance that alternative internship timelines are viable and valuable.

cta.observed_words: Use this summer to build skills network and use the step-by-step process I have on my page. Then go online on securing a fall internship
cta.requested_action: Build skills, network, and secure a fall internship using the speaker's process.

claims.observed_claim: Fall and spring internships are just as valuable as summer internships.
Fall and spring internships are less competitive and easier to land.
Employers do not care when you intern, only that you have the experience.

transferable_structure.hook_pattern: Urgent question or statement targeting a specific audience pain point.
transferable_structure.beat_pattern: Problem identification -> Alternative solution presentation -> Actionable advice.
transferable_structure.payoff_pattern: Reframing the problem as an opportunity and providing a clear next step.

evidence_limitations: No visual evidence of the speaker's page or process is shown.
Claims about competitiveness and employer preferences are not visually substantiated.

metadata.speech_exact: If you haven't gotten a summer internship yet,
you need to hear this. There's actually one secret
that almost no college students get told. Everyone thinks it's
summer internship or bust. That's just not true.
There are fall and spring internships that are honestly just as valuable.
A lot of cases they're actually better because they're less competitive,
so they're easier to land,
and you can usually get school credit or have them as part of a co op program.
You're not falling behind, you're actually just taking a different path.
So if you don't have something for summer right now,
keep trying. But if you actually end up not getting one,
then it's not the end of the world. This summer to build skills,
network and use the step by step process I have on my page.
Then go all in on securing a fall internship
because employers don't care when you enter your eventual full time job
will care that you actually did it and you can speak to it.
All you need is one. So lock in and best of luck!

opening.mechanism: Text overlay and direct eye contact

content_arc.progression: Using Claude AI to optimize the resume
content_arc.payoff: Receiving an interview call within 2 days

cta.requested_action: Read the caption for more details

claims.observed_claim: I uploaded my resume to Claude AI. 2 days later, I got an interview call.

transferable_structure.hook_pattern: Direct eye contact and text overlay with a compelling claim
transferable_structure.payoff_pattern: Encouraging viewers to read the caption for more details

metadata.camera_and_motion: Static camera, close-up framing of the subject's face.
metadata.on_screen_text_exact: Claude logo + LinkedIn logo at top center; 'I uploaded my resume to Claude AI. 2 days later, I got an interview call.' in white text box center; 'Here are the 7 prompts I used' in white text box below; 'Read the caption 👇' in white text box at bottom.
metadata.visual_description: A young man with light brown hair and a light blue t-shirt is shown in a close-up shot. He looks directly at the camera with a neutral expression. The background is a plain wall with a smoke detector visible. Text overlays and logos are present.

metadata.camera_and_motion: Static camera, medium shot framing of the subject.
metadata.on_screen_text_exact: Claude logo + LinkedIn logo at top center; 'I uploaded my resume to Claude AI. 2 days later, I got an interview call.' in white text box center; 'Here are the 7 prompts I used' in white text box below; 'Read the caption 👇' in white text box at bottom.
metadata.visual_description: The camera pulls back to a medium shot, revealing more of the man's upper body and the kitchen environment. He briefly looks to his right and then back at the camera, maintaining a neutral expression. The kitchen background includes white cabinets and a countertop with various items.

medium shot with text overlay

opening.observed_words: You're just trying to get one interview
opening.observed_visual: Soccer match with text overlay 'You're just trying to get one interview'

metadata.camera_and_motion: Static wide shot, high angle, no camera movement.
metadata.on_screen_text_exact: POV: You're just trying to get one interview (white text, top center).
metadata.visual_description: A wide shot of a soccer field with players in blue and white uniforms. The ball is in play near the center circle.

metadata.camera_and_motion: Zoom in, tracking shot following the player in blue.
metadata.on_screen_text_exact: POV: You're just trying to get one interview (white text, top center), Ghost Jobs (white text, center), ATS filters (white text, center).
metadata.visual_description: A player in a blue uniform dribbles the ball forward, evading a defender in white. The camera zooms in to focus on the player's movement.

metadata.camera_and_motion: Tracking shot, following the player's run towards the goal.
metadata.on_screen_text_exact: POV: You're just trying to get one interview (white text, top center), 500+ Applicants (white text, center), No Referral (white text, center), Auto-Rejection (white text, center).
metadata.visual_description: The player in blue continues to dribble towards the goal, passing several defenders. The camera follows his movement closely.

metadata.camera_and_motion: Tracking shot, following the player as he scores the goal.
metadata.on_screen_text_exact: POV: You're just trying to get one interview (white text, top center), 500+ Applicants (white text, center), Auto-Rejection (white text, center).
metadata.visual_description: The player in blue shoots the ball past the goalkeeper and into the net, scoring a goal. The camera follows the ball's trajectory.

Clean shot of goal celebration with no text overlays
